task: I’m comparing a couple of used car listings and one seller is pushing a fast deposit before I can see the full paperwork. Can you help me make a quick checklist of what I should verify first so I don’t miss anything important?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — if they’re asking for a fast deposit, verify the **paperwork and ownership basics first** before anything else.

Here’s a quick **must-check checklist**:

### 1) Seller + ownership
- Full name matches the title/registration
- Seller is the actual owner or legally authorized to sell
- ID matches the paperwork
- No signs of title jumping / third-party sale

### 2) Title status
- Clean title, salvage, rebuilt, flood, theft recovery, etc.
- Title is in hand and not “being mailed”
- VIN on title matches the car exactly

### 3) VIN verification
- VIN matches:
  - dashboard
  - door jamb sticker
  - title/registration
  - listing
- No signs of tampering or mismatched plates

### 4) Lien / loan payoff
- Any outstanding lien?
- If yes, get the payoff process in writing
- Confirm how title will be released after payment

### 5) Service / repair history
- Maintenance records
- Recent repairs and why they were done
- Any major issues disclosed: engine, transmission, electrical, airbags, frame, rust

### 6) Accident / damage history
- Prior accidents
- Frame or structural damage
- Airbag deployment
- Flood or fire damage

### 7) Registration + legal status
- Current registration
- Emissions / inspection status if applicable
- Any tickets, fees, or back taxes tied to the vehicle

### 8) Sale terms
- Total price
- Deposit amount
- Whether deposit is refundable
- Exact conditions for refund
- Written agreement before sending money

### 9) Inspection rights
- Can you see it in person first?
- Can you get a pre-purchase inspection?
- Can you verify it with a mechanic or a VIN report before deposit?

### 10) Red flags
- Pressure to act immediately
- Won’t share VIN or title photo
- Wants payment via irreversible method
- Story doesn’t match paperwork
- Refuses inspection or refuses to answer basic questions

If you want, I can turn this into a **very short copy-paste checklist** you can send the seller, or a **yes/no table** you can use while reviewing the listing.

user_simulator.text:
Pretty solid, but I’d want it tighter and more practical before I send money. Add:

- exact VIN and a photo of the title, front and back
- seller’s full name + matching ID
- whether there’s a lien, and if yes, lender name + payoff steps
- current mileage and whether it matches the listing
- service records or at least recent maintenance dates
- a pre-purchase inspection before any deposit
- deposit terms in writing: refundable or not, and what happens if paperwork doesn’t check out
- payment method: no Zelle/Cash App/crypto/wire unless I already verified everything

Also worth checking if the title is clean and in their name only. I’ve seen the “deposit first, paperwork later” thing go sideways before — not even worth rushing.

If you want, make it into a super short seller message I can paste.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter version you can send:

**Before I send any deposit, I need to verify:**
- Exact **VIN** + photo of the **title front and back**
- Seller’s **full name** + **ID** matching the title
- Whether there’s a **lien**; if yes, the **lender name** and payoff steps
- **Current mileage** and whether it matches the listing
- **Service records** or at least recent maintenance dates
- A **pre-purchase inspection** before any money changes hands
- **Deposit terms in writing**: refundable or not, and what happens if the paperwork doesn’t check out
- **Payment method** — I won’t use Zelle, Cash App, crypto, or wire until everything is verified
- Confirm the **title is clean and in the seller’s name only**

If you want, I can turn that into a **short, firm text message** you can paste to the seller.

user_simulator.text: Yep, that’s the version. Tight enough, practical, not too wordy. I can use that.
